← calib-targets-rs

Performance

Where the milliseconds go, measured per image on public sample boards — not generic aggregates. Cost is regime-dependent: corner detection dominates sparse boards, the owned grid build and decode take over on dense ones.

Per-image stage breakdown

Per-stage p50 from topo_stage_timing. RingFit vs DiskFit is shown only on the corner-detection row — it is purely a chess-corners orientation method and every downstream grid stage is orientation-agnostic. DiskFit roughly doubles corner detection, which is why RingFit is the default.
corner detection (external) grid build (owned) decode (owned)

End-to-end latency — public frames

Framep50 (ms)

Decode-sweep scaling — synthetic PuzzleBoard

Optimization roadmap — correctness-first, no false-positive trade

BottleneckEvidenceApproachStatus
